analysis_steps: Identified dominant peak at 517.27 cm⁻¹ as crystalline silicon first-order TO phonon, slightly downshifted from ideal 520.7 cm⁻¹
Confirmed silicon assignment with supporting peaks at 300.80 cm⁻¹ (2TA) and 969.37 cm⁻¹ (2TO second-order)
Identified 1579.94 cm⁻¹ peak as graphitic carbon G band, indicating sp2-bonded carbon presence
Identified 2670.19 cm⁻¹ peak as graphite/graphene 2D (2D') band, confirming graphitic carbon
Noted absence of D band (~1350 cm⁻¹) suggesting low-defect graphitic carbon, possibly graphene
Assessed 2D band intensity (11879) exceeds G band intensity (8887), with 2D/G ratio ~1.34, consistent with few-layer graphene
Concluded primary material is crystalline bulk silicon substrate with graphene/graphitic carbon contamination or intentional deposition
Assigned sample purity as contaminated since two distinct material signatures are present
decision_trace: Dominant peak at 517.27 cm⁻¹ matches crystalline Si first-order TO phonon (~520 cm⁻¹)
Peak at 300.80 cm⁻¹ matches Si 2TA overtone mode
Peak at 969.37 cm⁻¹ matches Si second-order 2TO mode (~960-970 cm⁻¹)
Peak at 1579.94 cm⁻¹ matches graphite/graphene G band (~1580 cm⁻¹)
Peak at 2670.19 cm⁻¹ matches graphite/graphene 2D band (~2700 cm⁻¹)
No D band (~1350 cm⁻¹) detected, suggesting well-ordered graphitic carbon
2D/G intensity ratio >1 suggests few-layer graphene on Si substrate
